'''Chris Bailey''' (born 5 July 1982), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Bails"''' and '''"Ice Ice Bailey"''', is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] and the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and the [[London Broncos]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], primarily as a {{rlp|fe}}, but also as a {{rlp|lk}}.

==Background==
Bailey was born in [[Inverell]], [[New South Wales]], Australia. He is the younger brother of former [[New South Wales state rugby league team|New South Wales]] and [[Australian Kangaroos|Australia]] representative forward [[Phil Bailey]].

==Career==
===National Rugby League===
Bailey made his [[NRL]] début for the [[Newcastle Knights]] against [[Wests Tigers]] at [[Campbelltown Stadium]] in round 10 of [[2006 NRL season|2006]].<ref name="Chris Bailey">{{cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/heraldsun/sport/nrl/player/0,27071,108542-104,00.html|title=Chris Bailey|publisher=Herald Sun|date=2008-05-16|access-date=2008-05-16|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080430121517/http://www.news.com.au/heraldsun/sport/nrl/player/0%2C27071%2C108542-104%2C00.html|archive-date=30 April 2008|url-status=dead}}</ref> Before his NRL début for the Knights, Bailey had been a junior for the [[Balmain Tigers]]. He was described by his [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] coach [[Brian Smith (rugby league, born 1954)|Brian Smith]] as an old-school {{rlp|FE}}.<ref name="Bailey barrage befits moment">{{cite web|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bailey-barrage-befits-moment/2008/04/21/1208629773901.html|title=Knights' Bailey barrage befits moment|publisher=LeagueHQ|date=2008-04-21|access-date=2008-05-16|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080424100253/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/bailey-barrage-befits-moment/2008/04/21/1208629773901.html|archive-date=24 April 2008 }}</ref>

He had been linked with a move to join his brother at [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] as a replacement for [[Trent Barrett]].<ref name="Knights' Bailey could be the key to unlocking Barrett deal">{{cite web|url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/knights-bailey-could-be-the-key-to-unlocking-barrett-deal/2008/05/16/1210765172869.html|title=Knights' Bailey could be the key to unlocking Barrett deal|publisher=LeagueHQ|date=2008-05-17|access-date=2008-05-16|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080519221735/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/knights-bailey-could-be-the-key-to-unlocking-barrett-deal/2008/05/16/1210765172869.html|archive-date=19 May 2008 }}</ref> but remained with the Knights until the end of the 2008 season.<ref name="Bailey to stay a Knight">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/b/bailey_chris/index.php|title=Knights' Bailey to stay a Knight|publisher=LeagueHQ|date=2006-07-17|access-date=2008-05-16|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080601034113/http://rleague.com/db/player/b/bailey_chris/index.php|archive-date=1 June 2008}}</ref>

Bailey joined the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], then the [[2008 NRL Grand Final|defending NRL premiers]], on a three-year deal from the [[2009 NRL season]].<ref name="Bailey seals deal with Manly">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,23744037-23214,00.html|title=Bailey seals deal with Manly|work=Fox Sports|date=2008-05-22|access-date=2008-05-22}}</ref><ref name="2009 NRL Player Movements">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|title=2009 NRL Player Movements|publisher=NRL Live|date=2008-10-04|access-date=2008-10-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081005172545/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|archive-date=5 October 2008 }}</ref> He played five-eighth for the Sea Eagles in their [[2009 World Club Challenge]] win over the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] winners, the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. Manly won the game 28–20 in front of 32,569 fans at [[Elland Road]] in [[Leeds]].

Other than the WCC win, Bailey's time at Manly was ultimately unsuccessful. The injury ravaged Sea Eagles failed to advance past the first week of the finals in both 2009 and [[2010 NRL season#Finals|2010]] and the emergence of young [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] five-eighth [[Kieran Foran]] in 2009 saw Bailey released by the club at the end of the 2010 season (ironically Manly, with most of its star players free from injury, would go on to win the [[2011 NRL Grand Final]] with Foran at 5/8).

===Super League===
After leaving Manly, Bailey joined [[Super League]] club [[London Broncos]] on a three-year deal from the [[Super League XVI|2011 season]]. He then joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a two-year contract from the start of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Huddersfield Giants: Chris Bailey signs from Broncos|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24330351|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=18 January 2015|date=30 September 2013}}</ref>
